name: Joan Rivers
blurb: A pioneer of women in stand-up, she rose to prominence in 1965 through The Tonight Show under Johnny Carson and in 1986 became the first woman to host a late-night network talk show.
bio: Joan Rivers was an American comedian and television host noted for a blunt, acerbic, heavily self-deprecating persona that took aim at celebrities, politicians, and herself. A pioneer of women in stand-up, she rose to prominence in 1965 through The Tonight Show under Johnny Carson and in 1986 became the first woman to host a late-night network talk show. She won an Emmy and a Grammy and built a decades-long career across stand-up, talk television, and red-carpet commentary.

timeline.ev: Breakthrough appearance on The Tonight Show with Johnny Carson
Becomes Carsons first permanent Tonight Show guest host
First woman to host her own late-night network talk show on Fox
Wins Daytime Emmy for The Joan Rivers Show
Documentary Joan Rivers: A Piece of Work released

accolades: First woman to host a late-night network talk show (1986)
Daytime Emmy for Outstanding Talk Show Host (1990)
Grammy for Best Spoken Word Album (2015, posthumous)
Television Academy Hall of Fame inductee (2017)
